mission.title: Install and Configure vLLM
mission.description: vLLM is a high-throughput and memory-efficient inference and serving engine for large language models (LLMs). It is ideal for applications requiring fast and scalable LLM serving, with a focus on efficient memory management and high performance. Key trade-offs include the need for specific hardware optimizations and potential complexity in setup for optimal performance.

steps.title: Add Helm repository for vLLM
steps.description:
This step adds the vLLM Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration, allowing you to install vLLM using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add vllm https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/releases/download/v0.19.1

```

> This command adds the vLLM repository with the specific release version.

steps.title: Install vLLM CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for vLLM to function properly in your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vllm-project/vllm/v0.19.1/deploy/crds/vllm.io_vllms.yaml

```

> This command applies the CRDs necessary for vLLM.

steps.title: Install vLLM using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs the vLLM application in the specified namespace using Helm, ensuring that you are using the correct version.

```bash

helm install vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --create-namespace --version 0.19.1

```

> This command installs vLLM in the 'vllm' namespace.

steps.title: Create an example Custom Resource (CR)
steps.description:
This step creates an example Custom Resource for vLLM to demonstrate its functionality.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: example-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-3'
  replicas: 2
EOF

```

> This command creates an example vLLM resource with a specified model and number of replicas.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: example-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-3'
  replicas: 2
EOF

steps.title: Verify the vLLM Custom Resource is ready
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the vLLM Custom Resource to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get vllm -n vllm

```

> This command lists the vLLM resources in the 'vllm' namespace to verify their status.

uninstall.title: Uninstall vLLM
uninstall.description:
This step removes the vLLM installation from your Kubernetes cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources and may lead to data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall vllm --namespace vllm

kubectl delete namespace vllm

```

> This command uninstalls the vLLM release from the specified namespace.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade vLLM
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your configurations and data. This step involves draining the nodes, upgrading the Helm release, and verifying the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --version 0.19.1

kubectl uncordon <node-name>

kubectl get vllm -n vllm

```

> Replace <node-name> with the name of the node where vLLM is running.
> This command upgrades vLLM to the specified version.
> Replace <node-name> with the name of the node to make it schedulable again.
> This command checks the status of the vLLM resources to ensure they are running correctly after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'vllm not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The vLLM Custom Resource may not have been created successfully.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the vLLM controller and ensure the CRD was applied correctly.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to the model server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The model server may not be running or is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Verify the model server's deployment status and configuration in the vLLM Custom Resource.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources to schedule pod'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources available for the vLLM pods.

**Fix:**
Check the resource requests and limits in the vLLM configuration and ensure the cluster has sufficient resources.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Helm release failed'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Helm installation may have encountered an issue due to misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Review the Helm installation logs for errors and ensure all required parameters are set correctly.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new model types and improved memory management features.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated older model configurations that are no longer supported.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v0.19.0, ensure to update your model configurations to align with the new specifications.

resolution.summary: You should see the vLLM resource listed with a status of 'Ready' when running 'kubectl get vllm -n vllm'.

EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the required resources and tools installed.
